Current: .dropdown-item has :hover/:focus-visible, plus .dropdown-destructive, .dropdown-label, .dropdown-separator, .dropdown-shortcut, .dropdown-checkbox-item/.dropdown-radio-item with ::before indicators. No rule references [disabled] or [data-disabled] anywhere in the file.
Gap: No disabled-item styling. shadcn DropdownMenuItem renders data-disabled with reduced opacity and pointer-events:none; shadcss items stay fully interactive/opaque when disabled.
Proposed CSS: @layer components { .dropdown-item:disabled, .dropdown-item[aria-disabled="true"], .dropdown-item[data-disabled] { pointer-events: none; opacity: 0.5; } }

Current: The summary has a :hover background and a transition, but there is NO :focus-visible rule anywhere in accordion.css. Keyboard users get only the UA default focus ring, not the shadcn ring token.
Gap: Accordion trigger (summary) has no visible keyboard focus ring. shadcn's AccordionTrigger applies a focus-visible ring. The sibling collapsible component DOES define .collapsible-trigger:focus-visible, so accordion is inconsistent and below parity.
Proposed CSS: .accordion > details > summary:focus-visible {;   outline: 2px solid hsl(var(--ring));;   outline-offset: -2px;; }

Current: .alert-dialog-footer is display:flex; gap; padding; with optional [data-justify="center"|"between"]. Default is a left-aligned single flex row, no mobile stacking and no default right-alignment.
Gap: shadcn's AlertDialogFooter defaults to a responsive layout: buttons stack vertically (reversed) on small screens and switch to a right-aligned horizontal row on sm+ breakpoints. shadcss's footer is a plain left-aligned row.
Proposed CSS: .alert-dialog-footer {;   flex-direction: column-reverse;;   justify-content: flex-end;; }; @media (min-width: 640px) {;   .alert-dialog-footer { flex-direction: row; }; }

Current: .badge styles layout/colors and .badge-dot adds a ::before dot. No rule targets child svg; no focus/link state.
Gap: shadcn's badge auto-sizes inline SVG icons (size-3) and sets pointer-events:none so leading icons render at a consistent small size. shadcss badge leaves svg at intrinsic size.
Proposed CSS: .badge > svg { width: 0.75rem; height: 0.75rem; flex-shrink: 0; pointer-events: none; }

Current: .btn sets icon support via gap and .btn-icon sizing, but has NO rule targeting child svg — no width/height or pointer-events on icons.
Gap: shadcn's button auto-sizes inline SVG icons (size-4) and sets pointer-events:none / shrink-0 so icons render consistently. shadcss buttons leave svg at intrinsic size.
Proposed CSS: .btn > svg { width: 1rem; height: 1rem; flex-shrink: 0; pointer-events: none; }

Current: Range styling via [data-range="start|end|middle"] only sets border-radius and accent fill (middle). The solid primary endpoint fill is only applied via [aria-pressed="true"].
Gap: Range endpoints (start/end) do not themselves carry the primary fill; shadcn's range_start/range_end days are filled with the primary color as the visual anchors without depending on a separate selected attribute.
Proposed CSS: .calendar-day[data-range="start"], .calendar-day[data-range="end"] { background: hsl(var(--primary)); color: hsl(var(--primary-foreground)); }

Current: .carousel-viewport uses scroll-snap-type: x mandatory and .carousel-track is display:flex (row). No vertical orientation support anywhere in the file.
Gap: No vertical orientation. shadcn carousel supports orientation="vertical", scrolling top-to-bottom with stacked items and up/down arrows.
Proposed CSS: .carousel[data-orientation="vertical"] .carousel-viewport { overflow-x: hidden; overflow-y: auto; scroll-snap-type: y mandatory; }; .carousel[data-orientation="vertical"] .carousel-track { flex-direction: column; }; .carousel[data-orientation="vertical"] .carousel-item { width: 100%; height: auto; scroll-snap-align: start; }; .carousel[data-orientation="vertical"] .carousel-controls { flex-direction: column; }

Current: Has .checkbox (native restyled), CSS-only checkmark, [data-indeterminate] mixed mark, plus :checked, :hover, :focus-visible, :disabled. No invalid/error state styling.
Gap: No aria-invalid (error) state. shadcn's Checkbox applies a destructive border (and destructive ring on focus) when aria-invalid is set. shadcss applies a destructive treatment on .input but not on .checkbox.
Proposed CSS: .checkbox[aria-invalid="true"] { border-color: hsl(var(--destructive)); }; .checkbox[aria-invalid="true"]:focus-visible { outline-color: hsl(var(--destructive)); }

Current: .command-item has :hover and [data-selected="true"] styling, plus svg color states. There is no disabled rule for .command-item.
Gap: No disabled command-item state. shadcn's CommandItem renders data-disabled and applies pointer-events-none + opacity-50 so unavailable commands are visibly inert.
Proposed CSS: .command-item[data-disabled="true"], .command-item:disabled { pointer-events: none; opacity: 0.5; }

Current: Provides .context-menu-item-check and .context-menu-item-arrow plus inherited dropdown-item styles, but no .context-menu-sub-content surface and no disabled styling.
Gap: Has a submenu arrow indicator but no sub-content panel class to style the nested menu surface; and no disabled-item state. shadcn ContextMenu provides SubContent and data-disabled styling.
Proposed CSS: @layer components { .context-menu-sub { min-width: 8rem; padding: var(--space-1); background: hsl(var(--popover)); color: hsl(var(--popover-foreground)); border: 1px solid hsl(var(--border)); border-radius: var(--radius-md); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); } .context-menu .dropdown-item:disabled, .context-menu .dropdown-item[aria-disabled="true"] { pointer-events: none; opacity: 0.5; } }

Current: drawer.css has .drawer, header/title/description/body/footer, four data-side variants with slide animations, backdrop. No drag-handle element.
Gap: shadcn Drawer (vaul) renders a visible drag handle bar near the top edge (the small rounded pill). shadcss has no equivalent styled handle part.
shadcn Ref: DrawerContent built-in handle (mx-auto mt-4 h-2 w-[100px] rounded-full bg-muted)
Proposed CSS: .drawer-handle {;   margin: var(--space-4) auto 0;;   height: 0.5rem;;   width: 100px;;   flex-shrink: 0;;   border-radius: var(--radius-full, 9999px);;   background: hsl(var(--muted));; }

Current: No submenu primitives exist. There is no .dropdown-sub-trigger or .dropdown-sub-content class; the file only handles a single flat menu level.
Gap: No submenu support. shadcn provides DropdownMenuSub/SubTrigger/SubContent (nested menu opening to the side with a chevron). shadcss has no sub-trigger chevron or sub-content surface.
Proposed CSS: @layer components { .dropdown-sub-trigger { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); width: 100%; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); font-size: var(--text-sm); border-radius: calc(var(--radius-md) - 2px); cursor: pointer; } .dropdown-sub-trigger:hover, .dropdown-sub-trigger[data-state="open"] { background: hsl(var(--accent)); color: hsl(var(--accent-foreground)); } .dropdown-sub-content { min-width: 8rem; padding: var(--space-1); background: hsl(var(--popover)); color: hsl(var(--popover-foreground)); border: 1px solid hsl(var(--border)); border-radius: var(--radius-md); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); } }

Current: `.field` is a single-column grid. There is no horizontal/row layout option; label always sits above the control.
Gap: shadcn's Field supports orientation="horizontal", which lays the label beside the control (used for switch/checkbox rows and settings forms). shadcss has no equivalent.
Proposed CSS: .field[data-orientation="horizontal"] {;   grid-template-areas:;     "control label"; "control description"; "control error";;   grid-template-columns: auto 1fr;;   align-items: center;;   column-gap: var(--space-3);; }

Current: Error visibility is driven entirely by :has(:user-invalid) / :has(:invalid:not(:placeholder-shown)) pseudo-class selectors. There is no attribute hook to force the invalid state.
Gap: shadcn's Field sets data-invalid (control gets aria-invalid) from the form library to drive the error display, independent of native validity. shadcss cannot surface errors for form-library-validated fields where the control isn't natively :invalid.
Proposed CSS: .field[data-invalid="true"] .field-error, .field:has([aria-invalid="true"]) .field-error { display: flex; }; .field[data-invalid="true"] .input, .field:has([aria-invalid="true"]) .input, .field[data-invalid="true"] .textarea, .field:has([aria-invalid="true"]) .textarea { border-color: hsl(var(--destructive)); }

Current: Invalid styling triggers only on `.input:invalid:not(:placeholder-shown)` and `.input:user-invalid`. There is no `[aria-invalid]` selector anywhere in input.css.
Gap: shadcn's Input keys its error state off aria-invalid (set by react-hook-form / Field), not native :invalid. A form library adding aria-invalid="true" gets no destructive styling in shadcss.
Proposed CSS: .input[aria-invalid="true"] { border-color: hsl(var(--destructive)); }; .input[aria-invalid="true"]:focus { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px hsl(var(--destructive) / 0.25); }

Current: .menubar-trigger has :hover and :focus-visible. shadcss only styles :hover, so the trigger does not stay highlighted while its menu is open.
Gap: No open-state styling on the trigger. shadcn MenubarTrigger applies bg-accent on data-state=open/data-highlighted; shadcss trigger reverts to transparent once the pointer leaves even though the popover stays open.
Proposed CSS: @layer components { .menubar-trigger[data-state="open"], .menubar-trigger[aria-expanded="true"] { background: hsl(var(--accent)); color: hsl(var(--accent-foreground)); } }

Current: Has .radio (native restyled, round, CSS dot), plus :checked, :hover, :focus-visible, :disabled. No invalid/error state styling.
Gap: No aria-invalid (error) state. shadcn's RadioGroupItem applies a destructive border + destructive focus ring when aria-invalid is set; shadcss has this convention on .input but not .radio.
Proposed CSS: .radio[aria-invalid="true"] { border-color: hsl(var(--destructive)); }; .radio[aria-invalid="true"]:focus-visible { outline-color: hsl(var(--destructive)); }

Current: Has .select (native restyled select with CSS chevron), .select-sm, .select-lg, plus :hover, :focus-visible, :disabled states. No invalid/error state styling.
Gap: No aria-invalid (error) state. shadcn's Select applies a destructive border + destructive focus ring when aria-invalid is set. shadcss uses this convention on .input but not .select.
Proposed CSS: .select[aria-invalid="true"] { border-color: hsl(var(--destructive)); }; .select[aria-invalid="true"]:focus-visible { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px hsl(var(--destructive) / 0.25); }

Current: Has header/brand/content/group/menu/menu-button/menu-badge/menu-sub/footer, collapsible icon, inset/floating variants, mobile drawer. No menu-action.
Gap: No SidebarMenuAction part — a trailing action button (e.g. ellipsis/more) absolutely positioned on a menu item, which shadcn provides and is commonly used for per-item menus.
shadcn Ref: SidebarMenuAction (absolute right-1 top-1.5 size-5, peer-hover/focus visible)
Proposed CSS: @layer components { .sidebar-menu-action { position: absolute; inset-inline-end: var(--space-1); top: 50%; transform: translateY(-50%); display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 1.25rem; height: 1.25rem; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); color: hsl(var(--sidebar-foreground)); background: transparent; cursor: pointer; } .sidebar-menu-action:hover { background: hsl(var(--sidebar-accent)); color: hsl(var(--sidebar-accent-foreground)); } }

Current: Slider styles the runnable track via linear-gradient driven by --slider-fill, plus thumb pseudo-elements, sizes, disabled, focus-visible, vertical orientation. The focusable element is the input itself, so :focus-visible on ::-webkit-slider-thumb never matches.
Gap: shadcn's slider thumb shows a focus ring when the thumb receives keyboard focus. In shadcss the keyboard focus ring on the thumb is effectively missing because the pseudo-element is not the focus target.
Proposed CSS: .slider:focus-visible::-webkit-slider-thumb { outline: 2px solid hsl(var(--ring)); outline-offset: 2px; } .slider:focus-visible::-moz-range-thumb { outline: 2px solid hsl(var(--ring)); outline-offset: 2px; }

Current: tabs-trigger styles base, :hover, :checked active, :focus-visible. No disabled state anywhere in tabs.css.
Gap: No disabled trigger state. A disabled tab should be dimmed and non-interactive.
Proposed CSS: @layer components {;   .tabs-list > input[type="radio"]:disabled + .tabs-trigger {;     opacity: 0.5;;     pointer-events: none;;     cursor: not-allowed;;   }; }

Current: Invalid styling triggers only on `.textarea:invalid:not(:placeholder-shown)` and `.textarea:user-invalid`. No `[aria-invalid]` selector present.
Gap: Same as input: shadcn's Textarea uses aria-invalid for the error state. Form-library-driven validation sets aria-invalid and would receive no destructive styling here.
Proposed CSS: .textarea[aria-invalid="true"] { border-color: hsl(var(--destructive)); }; .textarea[aria-invalid="true"]:focus { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px hsl(var(--destructive) / 0.25); }

Current: .toast has title, description, close, and variants success/destructive/warning/info. There is no action-button part; only the close button exists.
Gap: shadcn's Toast provides ToastAction — a styled alternative-action button rendered inline (e.g. "Undo", "Try again"). The sibling sonner.css has .sonner-action; toast.css omits any action slot.
Proposed CSS: @layer components {;   .toast-action {;     margin-inline-start: var(--space-2);;     flex-shrink: 0;;   }; .toast-action .btn {;     height: 1.75rem;;     padding: 0 var(--space-3);;     font-size: var(--text-xs);;   }; }

Current: Single kbd, .kbd rule: inline-flex chip, mono font, muted bg, border. No grouping wrapper. Registry classes: ['kbd'] only.
Gap: No group wrapper for rendering multi-key shortcuts (e.g. 'Ctrl + K') with consistent spacing/alignment. shadcn ships a KbdGroup alongside Kbd.
Proposed CSS: @layer components {;   .kbd-group {;     display: inline-flex;;     align-items: center;;     gap: 0.25rem;;     vertical-align: middle;;   }; .kbd-group .kbd, .kbd-group kbd { vertical-align: baseline; }; }

Current: accordion.css styles summary via :hover and [open] only. There is no rule keyed off a disabled state.
Gap: No disabled-state styling for an accordion item/trigger. shadcn dims and blocks interaction on disabled triggers.
Proposed CSS: .accordion > details[data-disabled] > summary, .accordion > details > summary:disabled {;   opacity: 0.5;;   pointer-events: none;;   cursor: not-allowed;; }

Current: breadcrumb, breadcrumb-item, breadcrumb-link, breadcrumb-current; separator via ::after. No ellipsis class in CSS or registry.
Gap: No ellipsis sub-part for collapsed trails. shadcn renders a '…' placeholder item (with sr-only 'More') between truncated crumbs.
shadcn Ref: BreadcrumbEllipsis (data-slot=breadcrumb-ellipsis, size-9 flex centered MoreHorizontal icon)
Proposed CSS: @layer components {;   .breadcrumb-ellipsis {;     display: inline-flex;;     align-items: center;;     justify-content: center;;     width: 1.25rem;;     height: 1.25rem;;     color: hsl(var(--muted-foreground));;   }; .breadcrumb-ellipsis > svg { width: 1rem; height: 1rem; }; }

Current: .btn-destructive sets background/hover only; focus-visible is the generic .btn:focus-visible outline of hsl(var(--ring)) for all variants.
Gap: shadcn's destructive button uses a destructive-colored focus ring so the focus indicator matches the variant. shadcss destructive uses the neutral ring color.
Proposed CSS: .btn-destructive:focus-visible { outline-color: hsl(var(--destructive) / 0.6); }

Current: .dropdown-item uses fixed padding; there is no inset modifier and no padding alignment for items beside checkbox/radio items.
Gap: No inset item variant. shadcn DropdownMenuItem/Label expose inset, adding left padding so plain items align with the text of checkbox/radio items.
Proposed CSS: @layer components { .dropdown-item-inset, .dropdown-label-inset { padding-inline-start: 2rem; } }

Current: .empty has .empty-icon (a 3rem circular badge), plus title, description, actions, and color variants. No default media slot.
Gap: shadcn's Empty exposes EmptyMedia with variant="default" (a larger, background-less media slot for images/avatars/oversized illustrations). shadcss only models the icon badge.
Proposed CSS: @layer components {;   .empty-media {;     display: flex;;     align-items: center;;     justify-content: center;;     margin-bottom: var(--space-2);;   }; .empty-media > svg { width: 1.5rem; height: 1.5rem; }; .empty-media img, .empty-media [data-slot="avatar"] { border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }; }

Current: .empty places title/description/actions as direct flow children with gap; there is no header grouping element.
Gap: shadcn's Empty groups media/title/description inside EmptyHeader, and body content inside EmptyContent (a flex column with its own gap/max-width). shadcss has .empty-actions but no general body wrapper.
Proposed CSS: @layer components {;   .empty-content {;     display: flex;;     flex-direction: column;;     align-items: center;;     gap: var(--space-2);;     width: 100%;;     max-width: 20rem;;     margin-top: var(--space-2);;     font-size: var(--text-sm);;   }; }

Current: field.css exposes only .field, .field-label, .field-control, .field-description, .field-error. There is no grouping/stacking container for multiple fields.
Gap: shadcn provides FieldGroup — a flex-column container that adds consistent vertical spacing between sibling Fields. shadcss has no group wrapper.
Proposed CSS: @layer components {;   .field-group {;     display: flex;;     flex-direction: column;;     gap: var(--space-5);;   }; }

Current: No fieldset/legend primitives exist; the only label part is .field-label scoped to a single control.
Gap: shadcn provides FieldSet + FieldLegend for grouping related fields under a group caption, semantically a <fieldset>/<legend>. shadcss has no styled equivalent.
Proposed CSS: @layer components {;   .field-set { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: var(--space-4); border: none; margin: 0; padding: 0; }; .field-legend { font-size: var(--text-sm); font-weight: 500; color: hsl(var(--foreground)); padding: 0; }; }

Current: No separator primitive; fields are separated only by grid gap when a group wrapper is used.
Gap: shadcn provides FieldSeparator — an optional divider (sometimes with centered text) between field sections. shadcss has nothing to render a between-fields rule.
Proposed CSS: @layer components {;   .field-separator { height: 1px; background: hsl(var(--border)); border: none; margin-block: var(--space-2); }; }

Current: otp-input has default, :focus, :disabled, :user-invalid, plus separator and size wrappers. There is no active/caret indicator for the currently-focused slot.
Gap: shadcn input-otp renders a blinking caret in the active slot. shadcss relies on the native text caret of the focused input, which is functionally equivalent, so this is largely covered. No strong missing feature.
Proposed CSS: .otp-input:focus { position: relative; z-index: 10; }

Current: .navigation-menu-trigger and .navigation-menu-link style :hover and :focus-visible. No rule references data-active, aria-current, or data-state for an active/current link.
Gap: No active/current state. shadcn NavigationMenuLink supports data-active (highlights the current route with bg-accent/font-medium); shadcss has no current-page highlight.
Proposed CSS: @layer components { .navigation-menu-link[data-active], .navigation-menu-link[aria-current="page"], .navigation-menu-trigger[data-active] { background: hsl(var(--accent)); color: hsl(var(--accent-foreground)); } }

Current: pagination-prev/next only set color: muted-foreground and an aria-disabled opacity rule. No gap, no padding/label layout, no chevron sizing.
Gap: Previous/Next links lack the labelled button layout shadcn gives them (icon + 'Previous'/'Next' text with gap and padding). As-is they render as muted text without button affordance.
shadcn Ref: PaginationPrevious / PaginationNext (buttonVariants ghost, gap-1 px-2.5, ChevronLeft/Right + label)
Proposed CSS: @layer components {;   .pagination-prev, .pagination-next { gap: var(--space-1); padding-inline: var(--space-3); color: hsl(var(--foreground)); }; .pagination-prev > svg, .pagination-next > svg { width: 1rem; height: 1rem; }; }

Current: No separator class in CSS or registry; sidebar-footer uses its own border-top. Groups separated only by gap.
Gap: No SidebarSeparator part for a horizontal rule between sections.
Proposed CSS: @layer components {;   .sidebar-separator { height: 1px; margin-inline: var(--space-2); background: hsl(var(--sidebar-border)); border: 0; }; }

Current: sidebar-menu-button has a single fixed size. No size modifier classes or data-size attribute.
Gap: No menu-button size variants. shadcn SidebarMenuButton supports size='sm' and size='lg' (e.g. lg for a tall brand/profile row with two-line text).
Proposed CSS: @layer components {;   .sidebar-menu-button[data-size="sm"] { padding: var(--space-1) var(--space-2); font-size: var(--text-xs); }; .sidebar-menu-button[data-size="lg"] { padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); min-height: 3rem; }; }

Current: Has .prose (element styles) plus .prose .lead. Registry classes: ['prose','lead']. No standalone large/small/muted text helpers.
Gap: Missing the standalone typography text-style helpers shadcn's typography docs provide: 'large', 'small', and 'muted'. shadcss only has .lead.
Proposed CSS: @layer components {;   .prose .large, .text-large { font-size: var(--text-lg); font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.4; }; .prose .small, .text-small { font-size: var(--text-sm); font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; }; .prose .muted, .text-muted { font-size: var(--text-sm); color: hsl(var(--muted-foreground)); }; }
